similarity.label_zh: 互联网泡沫顶前 9 个月
互联网泡沫顶前 1 个月

summary.verdict_desc: 本周计数 6 红 / 7 黄 / 12 绿，红灯比例 24.0%、加权风险分 38.0%，两项头条读数与上周分毫不差——但平静的头条之下是一次实质的内部轮动，一进一出、恰好对冲。边际恶化来自市场结构：AI 龙头内部人卖压本周转为决定性红灯。LevelFields 记录近期 5 家科技龙头内部人卖出合计约 $688M（Broadcom Samueli $250M、NVDA Stevens $186M、Oracle Henley $63.7M、Palantir CEO Karp 约 89.1 万股 + COO Sankar 约 33.4 万股、CoreWeave 在列），而买盘『几近归零』（NVDA 过去一年零内部人买入、AI 板块 12 个月内部人处置约 $4.6B、三年净卖约 $15.6B），买卖比远超 20x 红线。此前三期因始终拿不到 12 家 30 日聚合美元比而沿用约 8x（标记疑似静态），本期据新披露的具名交易正式改判红灯并撤下静态标记——这使内部人指标向 2000-02（顶前 1 个月，当时约 23x）向量收敛了一格。边际改善来自宏观：Fed 隐含加息预期本周逆转。CME FedWatch（7/18）显示 7/29 FOMC 约 86.7% 维持、12 月约 78.2% 维持 / 15.4% 降息 / 仅 5.4% 加息（上周曾隐含约 77% 12 月前加息）；通胀仍粘（5 月 CPI 4.2%、higher-for-longer），政策向量由『隐含加息』回落至『维持高利率(通胀压力)』，由红降黄——这一格反而与 2000-02（当时 Fed 明确加息）拉开距离。两相抵消，动量净变化 net 0（1 恶化 / 1 好转），未触发动量提示阈值。两轴判读：泡沫成熟度 stage 维持 60.0（亢奋 Euphoria——估值、VC 集中度、vendor financing 依旧泡沫化），破裂临近度 trigger 维持 23.3（引线未燃——内部人转红被 Fed 转黄对冲）。一句话：『泡沫仍大、引线仍未燃，但顶部确认清单本周划掉一项（内部人）、又新增一项分歧（Fed）』。估值端极端未改：CAPE 约 41.3（multpl 41.52 @7/17、GuruFocus 41.12），逼近 2000 峰值 44.2；S&P 前 5 大权重约 26-28%（slickcharts 实时源受限，二手读数 25.8% 与 28.4% 并存，均压 25% 红线上方）；唯 NVDA 远期 PE 约 22.7x 维持绿灯——EPS 上修使龙头倍数随股价走低。资金面仍是最大压力源（类别分 87.5、3 红）：Mag4 自由现金流深度转负（Amazon 2026 FCF -$17B~-$28B、Alphabet 骤降约 90% 至 $8.2B，行业 capex 料 Q3 超经营现金流）、AI 占全球 VC 约 70%（美国 H1 86%、OpenAI+Anthropic 两家 $217B 占 43%）、NVDA 客户投资/收入约 43%（$40B+ 承诺 / $300B LTM，7/2 新推创业公司算力分成计划再添循环融资口实）。信用面继续最强支撑（类别分 12.5）：HY 利差约 269bps（Convex 7/10，较上周 268 基本持平，远低于 350 阈值）、数据中心 ABS 顶级券续收窄约 60bps、Neocloud 90 日零信用事件（CoreWeave 股价月内约 -35%、Meta 自建云施压，但 S&P 上调展望至正面、属股权而非信用）。市场结构类别分因内部人转红由 50.0 升至 66.7。基本面持续兑现（类别分 0）：OpenRouter 约 100T/月（周约 25T、8M+ 用户，但绝对量与上期持平、+35% 环比已连续 4 期沿用，本期标记 token_volume_mom 疑似静态待人工核查）、Anthropic ARR 报 $47B（5 月，FY26 预测 $75-90B）领先 OpenAI 约 $33B、二阶导仍正、Oracle RPO $638B（+363%）叠加 MSFT $627B 令大三积压 >$1.7T、H100 现货中位约 $3.15/hr 且半数售罄、前沿进展在轨（METR 约 4.3 月倍增，Claude Fable 5 刷新 HLE 53.3% / SWE-bench Pro 80.3%、GPT-5.6 于 7/9 发布）。宏观情绪：Fed 降黄（见上）；capex 市场反应维持黄灯（Mag7 Q2 财报 7/22-30 未出，最新 Q1 为选择性惩罚，本周 TSMC Q2 全面超预期却仍遭 AI 芯片抛售、呈 beat-and-sell-off）；CEO 对冲维持黄灯（Nadella 7/13 警示 AI 或失 social permission、Zuckerberg 7/2 承认 AI agent 未达预期加速、Altman 称小盘估值 insane、Dimon 称 bubble territory，但均坚持长期兑现、未集体收手）。判读：基础档位按 red_pct 24.0%（<25%）本应为『观察期』，但分类强制升级再次生效——估值 2 红（CAPE、Top-5）叠加资金面 3 红（FCF、VC AI 占比、NVDA 客户投资），估值 + 资金面双类共振触发分类强制升级，维持『高风险预警』。两轴升级条件（stage≥60 且 trigger≥50）因 trigger 仅 23.3 未触发。历史相似度：最像 1999-06（顶前 9 个月，76%，因内部人本期转红、与 1999 年中的温和减持拉开一格而较上周略降）；其次 2000-02（顶前 1 个月，74%）与 2021-11（成长股/SPAC 顶，74%）并列——内部人转红使当前向量向 2000-02 靠拢；2007-10（金融危机股顶，41%）最不像。与 2000-02（顶前 1 个月）向量的主要差异，本周由五项收敛为四项：信用利差未走阔（269bps vs 走阔）、市场广度未确认崩溃（约 54% vs 崩，但 7/17 指数跌破自身 50 日均线、SOX 较高点 -20% 为新预警）、IPO 未成洪流（Anthropic 已进入 roadshow 但临近发行名单仍 <10）、NVDA 远期 PE 仍便宜（22.7x vs 泡沫化）——内部人减持这一项本周已不再是差异（双方皆红），它正是从『1999 年中』滑向『2000 年初』的确认信号之一，值得高度重视；另需并记的是债务/capex 比仍中性（55% vs 顶部 80-100%），且本周新增一项分歧——Fed 由加息转为维持（vs 2000-02 明确加息）。分母提醒：N=25 下 6 红对应 24.0%，跨期趋势以方向为主。（动量净 0，未触发提示阈值。）
summary.verdict_desc_en: This week prints 6 red / 7 yellow / 12 green, with the red share at 24.0% and the weighted risk score at 38.0% — both headline reads identical to last week, yet beneath the calm sits a real internal rotation, one in and one out that happens to cancel. The marginal deterioration comes from market structure: AI insider selling turned decisively red this week. LevelFields logged ~$688M of recent insider sales across five tech leaders (Broadcom's Samueli ~$250M, NVDA's Stevens ~$186M, Oracle's Henley ~$63.7M, Palantir CEO Karp ~891k shares plus COO Sankar ~334k, with CoreWeave in the group), while buying has 'all but vanished' (NVDA has logged zero insider purchases in a year; the AI complex has ~$4.6B of 12-month insider disposals and ~$15.6B of net selling over three years) — a sell/buy ratio well above the 20x red line. The prior three weeks carried ~8x (flagged suspect-static) because the 12-name 30-day aggregate dollar ratio was unobtainable; this week the newly surfaced named transactions justify a formal red and the removal of the static flag — nudging the insider vector one notch toward February 2000 (one month before the top, when it hit ~23x). The marginal improvement comes from macro: the Fed's implied-hike thesis reversed this week. CME FedWatch (July 18) shows ~86.7% hold at the July 29 FOMC and, for December, ~78.2% hold / 15.4% cut / just 5.4% hike (versus last week's implied ~77% chance of a hike by December); with inflation still sticky (May CPI 4.2%, higher-for-longer), the policy vector eased from 'implied hike' back to 'higher-for-longer hold (inflation pressure)', red to yellow — a notch that actually widens the gap to February 2000 (when the Fed was clearly hiking). The two offset, leaving net momentum 0 (1 deterioration / 1 improvement), below every momentum-alert threshold. Two-axis read: bubble maturity 'stage' holds at 60.0 (Euphoria — valuations, VC concentration and vendor financing remain bubble-grade), while rupture proximity 'trigger' holds at 23.3 (fuse unlit — the insider red is offset by the Fed yellow). In one line: the bubble is still large and the fuse still unlit, but the top-confirmation checklist crossed off one item (insiders) and added one new divergence (the Fed) this week. Valuations stay extreme: CAPE ~41.3 (multpl 41.52 on July 17, GuruFocus 41.12), edging toward the 2000 peak of 44.2; the S&P Top-5 weight is ~26-28% (the live Slickcharts source was blocked, with secondary reads of 25.8% and 28.4% both above the 25% red line); only NVDA's ~22.7x forward PE stays green, as EPS upgrades pull the leader's multiple down. Capital remains the biggest pressure point (category 87.5, three reds): Mag4 free cash flow is deeply negative (Amazon 2026 FCF -$17B to -$28B, Alphabet down ~90% to $8.2B, sector capex set to exceed operating cash flow by Q3), AI is ~70% of global VC (86% of US H1, OpenAI and Anthropic alone $217B, 43% of the half), and NVDA customer-financing runs ~43% of revenue ($40B+ committed against ~$300B LTM, with a new July 2 startup compute revenue-share program adding to the circular-financing critique). Credit remains the strongest support (category 12.5): HY OAS ~269bps (Convex July 10, essentially flat vs last week's 268 and far below the 350 threshold), top-rated data-center ABS tightened another ~60bps, and neocloud credit logged zero events in 90 days (CoreWeave equity is down ~35% on the month under Meta-compute pressure, but S&P revised its outlook to positive — an equity move, not credit). The market-structure category score rose from 50.0 to 66.7 on the insider red. Fundamentals keep delivering (category 0): OpenRouter is ~100T/month (~25T/week, 8M+ users), but with absolute volume flat versus last period the +35% MoM has now been carried for four straight weeks, so token_volume_mom is flagged suspect-static for manual review; Anthropic's ARR is reported at $47B (May, with FY26 projected $75-90B) leading OpenAI's ~$33B, the 2nd derivative still positive; Oracle RPO is $638B (+363%) which with Microsoft's $627B pushes big-3 backlog above $1.7T; H100 spot medians ~$3.15/hr with half of providers sold out; and frontier progress stays on-trend (METR doubling ~every 4.3 months, Claude Fable 5 resetting HLE to 53.3% and SWE-bench Pro to 80.3%, GPT-5.6 shipping July 9). Macro/sentiment: the Fed eases to yellow (above); the capex reaction holds yellow (Mag7 Q2 earnings land July 22-30, so the freshest read is Q1's selective punishment, while TSMC's Q2 beat-and-sell-off this week echoes the 'show me the returns' tape); CEO hedging holds yellow (Nadella on July 13 warned AI could lose its 'social permission', Zuckerberg on July 2 conceded AI agents have not accelerated as expected, Altman calls small-cap valuations 'insane' and Dimon 'bubble territory' — yet all insist AI pays off long term and none has collectively pulled back). Verdict: the base framework reads Observation on red_pct of 24.0% (<25%), but the category override fires again — valuation 2 red (CAPE, Top-5) plus capital 3 red (FCF, VC AI share, NVDA customer-financing), so valuation + capital dual-category resonance forces the upgrade, holding the verdict at High Risk Alert. The two-axis upgrade (stage>=60 and trigger>=50) does not fire because trigger is only 23.3. Historical similarity: today most resembles June 1999 (nine months before the dot-com top, 76%, a touch lower than last week as the insider red pulls away from 1999's mild selling), then February 2000 (one month before the top, 74%) tied with November 2021 (Growth/SPAC top, 74%) — the insider red moves the current vector toward February 2000; October 2007 (GFC equity top, 41%) is least alike. The gap versus the February 2000 vector narrowed from five items to four this week: credit spreads have not widened (269bps vs widening), breadth has not confirmed a collapse (~54% vs a break — though the index slipping below its own 50-day MA on July 17 and the SOX down ~20% from highs are new warnings), the IPO pipeline is not a flood (Anthropic has entered its roadshow but imminent names are still under 10), and NVDA's forward PE is still cheap (22.7x vs bubble-grade) — with insider selling no longer a difference (both red now), which is precisely one of the confirmation signals for a slide from 'mid-1999' to 'early-2000' and warrants close attention; also worth noting, debt/capex is still moderate (55% vs an 80-100% peak), and a new divergence appeared this week as the Fed shifted from hiking to holding (vs a clearly-hiking February 2000). Denominator reminder: with N=25 the six reds map to 24.0%, so cross-period trend comparison should lean on direction. (Net momentum 0, below every alert threshold.)

wow_changes.note: 内部人卖压转为决定性红灯——近期 5 家科技龙头内部人卖出合计约 $688M（Broadcom Samueli $250M、NVDA Stevens $186M、Oracle Henley $63.7M、Palantir Karp 约 89.1 万股等），买盘几近归零（NVDA 一年零买入）；卖买比远超 20x 红线，为本轮首次触红，据新披露具名交易改判、不再沿用约 8x 静态值
wow_changes.note_en: Insider selling turned decisively red — ~$688M of recent insider sales across five tech leaders (Broadcom's Samueli $250M, NVDA's Stevens $186M, Oracle's Henley $63.7M, Palantir's Karp ~891k shares, and more) with buying all but gone (NVDA zero purchases in a year); the ratio is well above the 20x red line, its first red this cycle — re-rated on newly disclosed named transactions rather than the carried ~8x static value

wow_changes.note: Fed 隐含加息预期本周逆转——CME FedWatch（7/18）显示 12 月加息概率由上周约 77% 骤降至约 5%，约 78% 维持 / 15% 降息；政策向量由『隐含加息』回落至『维持高利率(通胀压力)』，由红降黄（5 月 CPI 4.2% 仍粘）
wow_changes.note_en: The Fed's implied-hike expectation reversed this week — CME FedWatch (July 18) shows the December hike probability collapsing from ~77% last week to ~5%, with ~78% hold / 15% cut; the policy vector eased from 'implied hike' to 'higher-for-longer hold (inflation pressure)', red to yellow (May CPI still sticky at 4.2%)

indicators.threshold_text_en: >35 red / 25-35 yellow / <25 green
indicators.note: CAPE 约 41.3（multpl 41.52 @7/17、GuruFocus 41.12 @7/1、其它 41.37/41.66），较上周约 41.5 基本持平微降，仍逼近 2000 年峰值 44.2（仅差约 7%），远高于长期均值约 32、中位数约 16，估值处极度拉伸区，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: CAPE is ~41.3 (multpl 41.52 on July 17, GuruFocus 41.12 on July 1, others 41.37/41.66), essentially flat-to-slightly-lower than last week's ~41.5 and only ~7% below the 2000 peak of 44.2 — far above its ~32 long-run average and ~16 median; valuations remain extremely stretched, stays red

indicators.threshold_text_en: >25% red / 18-25% yellow / <25% green
indicators.note: slickcharts 实时源本周 egress 受限，二手读数并存：一组约 25.8%（前 10 大 36.4%，7/2 口径）、另一组（维基/Wikipedia 快照）约 28.4%（NVDA 7.32/AAPL 7.04/GOOGL 5.89/MSFT 4.50/AMZN 3.69），两者均压在 25% 红线上方，集中度仍处历史高位，市值高度押注少数 AI 龙头，维持红灯（取中值约 27%，待人工核对 slickcharts）
indicators.note_en: The live Slickcharts source was egress-blocked this week; two secondary reads coexist — one at ~25.8% (Top-10 36.4%, July 2 basis) and one (Wikipedia snapshot) at ~28.4% (NVDA 7.32 / AAPL 7.04 / GOOGL 5.89 / MSFT 4.50 / AMZN 3.69) — both above the 25% red line, with concentration historically elevated and the index heavily bet on a few AI leaders; stays red (midpoint ~27%, pending manual Slickcharts check)

indicators.threshold_text_en: >40 red / 30-40 yellow / <30 green
indicators.note: NVDA 远期 PE 约 22.7x（GuruFocus 22.72 @7/18、FinanceCharts 24.04、StockAnalysis 20.34，区间约 20-24x），较半导体行业中位数约 35 显著折价；EPS 持续上修使倍数随股价走低（价涨而 PE 反降），NVDA 估值并非泡沫源头，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: NVDA's forward PE is ~22.7x (GuruFocus 22.72 on July 18, FinanceCharts 24.04, StockAnalysis 20.34; ~20-24x range), a meaningful discount to the ~35 semiconductor median; ongoing EPS upgrades pull the multiple down even as the stock rises — NVDA's valuation is not the bubble's source, stays green

indicators.name_zh: AI 私募二级市场标价
indicators.threshold_text: 折价/下跌=红 / 溢价收窄+卖压=黄 / 溢价稳定或扩大=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Discount/falling = red / Narrowing premium + sell pressure = yellow / Stable-to-wider premium = green
indicators.note: 二级标价持续走强但内部分化：Anthropic 隐含估值约 $1.2T（Caplight，+550% YoY，对 $965B Series H 溢价约 24%），Hiive 上 3 个月 +211% 至约 $900，卖方近乎绝迹；OpenAI 约 $908B（Caplight）/ $880B（Hiive，对 $852B primary 溢价约 3-7%），Hiive 3 月仅 +8.5%，明显慢于 Anthropic。旗舰均无折价、溢价扩大，一级市场无退潮信号，维持绿灯（领跑者由 OpenAI 轮动至 Anthropic）
indicators.note_en: Secondary marks keep firming but diverge internally: Anthropic's implied valuation is ~$1.2T (Caplight, +550% YoY, ~24% above its $965B Series H), up ~211% over three months on Hiive to ~$900, with sellers all but absent; OpenAI is ~$908B (Caplight) / $880B (Hiive, ~3-7% above its $852B primary) but up only ~8.5% over three months on Hiive, clearly slower than Anthropic. No flagship trades at a discount and premiums are widening — no retreat signal, stays green (leadership rotated from OpenAI to Anthropic)

indicators.threshold_text: 指引下调=红 / 加速=黄 / 稳健=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Guidance cut = red / Accelerating = yellow / Steady = green
indicators.note: 四大 hyperscaler 2026 capex 合计约 $725B（AMZN $200B、MSFT $190B、GOOGL $175-185B、META $125-145B，META 因存储涨价上修），YoY 约 +77%（对 2025 约 $410B），无一下调指引、财报窗口前均重申或上调；UBS 预计增速 2027 降至 +25%、2028 +6%（前方是放缓非收缩）。绝对增速仍在加速、不可持续特征明显，维持黄灯（Q2 财报 7/22-30 前沿用最新指引）
indicators.note_en: The four hyperscalers guide ~$725B of 2026 capex (AMZN $200B, MSFT $190B, GOOGL $175-185B, META $125-145B, Meta raised on memory-chip prices), ~+77% YoY (vs ~$410B in 2025), with none cutting and all reaffirming or raising ahead of earnings; UBS sees growth moderating to +25% in 2027 and +6% in 2028 (slowdown ahead, not a cut). The pace is still accelerating with clear unsustainability hallmarks — stays yellow (latest guidance carried ahead of Q2 prints July 22-30)

indicators.threshold_text_en: <-20% red / -20%~0 yellow / >0 green
indicators.note: 自由现金流被 capex 深度吞噬：Amazon 2026 全年 FCF 料转负（Morgan Stanley -$17B、BofA -$28B，$200B capex vs 约 $139.5B 经营现金流），Alphabet FCF 骤降约 90% 至约 $8.2B（Pivotal，$175-185B capex 约等于 $164.7B 经营现金流），Epoch 预计行业 capex 于 Q3 超经营现金流；Mag7 由造现金转向吞现金、需外部融资，维持红灯（Q2 财报前沿用最新估算）
indicators.note_en: Free cash flow is being deeply consumed by capex: Amazon's 2026 FCF is set to turn negative (Morgan Stanley -$17B, BofA -$28B; $200B capex vs ~$139.5B operating cash flow), Alphabet's FCF is plunging ~90% to ~$8.2B (Pivotal; $175-185B capex roughly equals $164.7B operating cash flow), and Epoch sees sector capex exceeding operating cash flow by Q3; the Mag7 have shifted from generating cash to consuming it, leaning on external financing — stays red (latest estimates carried ahead of Q2 prints)

indicators.name_zh: AI 占 VC 投资比重
indicators.threshold_text_en: >50% red / 30-50% yellow / <30% green
indicators.note: Crunchbase/PitchBook H1 2026：AI 占全球创投超 70%（一年前不足 50%）、占美国创投达 86%（$355.9B）；H1 全球创投创纪录 $510B（超 2025 全年 $440B），OpenAI+Anthropic 两家占 H1 全球 43%（$217B，Anthropic 上季 $65B、已成最有价值私营公司）；美元高度集中于少数巨额轮次，远超 50% 红线，维持红灯（H1 报告 7 月初发布，为最新硬数据）
indicators.note_en: Crunchbase/PitchBook H1 2026: AI took >70% of global VC (up from under 50% a year ago) and 86% of US VC ($355.9B); record H1 global VC of $510B (above all of 2025's $440B), with OpenAI and Anthropic alone at 43% of the global half ($217B; Anthropic raised $65B last quarter and is now the most valuable private company); dollars concentrate in a few mega-rounds far above the 50% red line — stays red (H1 reports published early July, the freshest hard data)

indicators.threshold_text_en: >30% red / 15-30% yellow / <15% green (Lucent '99 peak: 24%)
indicators.note: NVDA 2026 年 AI 股权承诺逾 $40B（OpenAI $30B 直投 + Corning 至多 $3.2B、IREN 至多 $2.1B、CoreWeave/Marvell/Lumentum/Coherent 等），占约 $300B LTM 收入约 43%，远超 Lucent 1999 峰值 24%；连续红灯，7/2 新推创业公司算力『营收分成』计划让『为自身供应链融资+锁客』的循环特征再受质疑，承诺额环比未见收敛，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: NVDA committed $40B+ of AI equity in 2026 ($30B direct into OpenAI plus up to $3.2B Corning, up to $2.1B IREN, and CoreWeave / Marvell / Lumentum / Coherent), ~43% of ~$300B LTM revenue — far above Lucent's 1999 peak of 24%; a persistent red, and a new July 2 startup 'revenue-share' compute program renewed scrutiny of its 'financing its own supply chain while locking in customers' loop, with no sequential narrowing — stays red

indicators.threshold_text_en: <40% red / 40-60% yellow / >60% green
indicators.note: 本周未获 S5FI 现值读数（barchart/macromicro 等数据源 egress 受限），沿用上周约 54.1%（最近可信读数为 6/18 约 55.3%），仍落 40-60% 黄区；但新增结构性预警：S&P 500 指数本身于 7/17 首次跌破自身 50 日均线、SOX 较高点约 -20%、AI 硬件/存储轮动流出，广度或已走弱，待下期硬数据确认，维持黄灯（数据源暂不可用）
indicators.note_en: No current S5FI read was obtainable this week (barchart/macromicro and other data hosts were egress-blocked), so last week's ~54.1% is carried (the most recent clean read was ~55.3% on June 18), still inside the 40-60% yellow band; but a new structural warning appeared — the S&P 500 index itself slipped below its own 50-day MA for the first time on July 17, the SOX is ~20% off its highs, and money rotated out of AI hardware/memory, hinting breadth may have weakened, pending next week's hard data — stays yellow (source unavailable)

indicators.name_zh: AI 龙头内部人卖买比
indicators.threshold_text_en: >20x = red / 5-20x = yellow / <5x = green (2000 peak: 23x)
indicators.note: 本周内部人卖压转为决定性红灯：LevelFields 记录近期 5 家科技龙头内部人卖出合计约 $688M（Broadcom Samueli $250M、NVDA Stevens $186M、Oracle Henley $63.7M、Palantir CEO Karp 约 89.1 万股 + COO Sankar 约 33.4 万股、CoreWeave 在列），买盘『几近归零』（NVDA 过去一年零买入、AI 板块 12 个月处置约 $4.6B、三年净卖约 $15.6B）。买盘归零使卖买比远超 20x 红线——本期据新披露的具名交易正式改判红灯、撤下前三期约 8x 静态标记；12 家 30 日聚合美元比仍无法精确获取，值 25x 为方向性估算
indicators.note_en: Insider selling turned decisively red this week: LevelFields logged ~$688M of recent insider sales across five tech leaders (Broadcom's Samueli $250M, NVDA's Stevens $186M, Oracle's Henley $63.7M, Palantir CEO Karp ~891k shares plus COO Sankar ~334k, with CoreWeave in the group), while buying has 'all but vanished' (NVDA zero purchases in a year, ~$4.6B of 12-month AI-complex disposals, ~$15.6B of net selling over three years). With buying near zero the ratio blows past the 20x red line — re-rated to red on the newly disclosed named transactions, removing the prior three weeks' ~8x static flag; the 12-name 30-day aggregate dollar ratio remains unobtainable, so the 25x value is a directional estimate

indicators.threshold_text: 洪流=红 / 升温=黄 / 平静=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Flood = red / Warming = yellow / Quiet = green
indicators.note: 管道继续升温但未成洪流：Anthropic 由『已递交 S-1』进展至 roadshow——银行/投资者路演本周（7/15-16）展开、瞄准 10 月上市（$965B post、约 $69B ARR）；DeepSeek 新入列（拟今年在华报 IPO、料 2027 上市）；OpenAI（confidential S-1 已交）与 Databricks 仍指向 2027。临近发行（6 个月内）名单实质仍只 Anthropic 一家、总数 <10，窗口谨慎，维持黄灯（本周边际继续升温）
indicators.note_en: The pipeline keeps warming but is short of a flood: Anthropic advanced from 'S-1 filed' to roadshow — banker/investor meetings began this week (July 15-16), targeting an October listing ($965B post, ~$69B ARR); DeepSeek is newly in the queue (readying a China IPO filing this year, debut ~2027); OpenAI (confidential S-1 filed) and Databricks still point to 2027. Imminent (within-6-month) names are still essentially just Anthropic, under 10 total, so the window stays cautious — stays yellow (marginally warmer this week)

indicators.threshold_text_en: >500 red / 350-500 yellow / <350 green
indicators.note: ICE BofA HY OAS 约 269bps（Convex 7/10 报 269、TradingEconomics 269、govspending 7/16 报 271；FRED 主源 egress 受限，取 FRED 镜像），较上周约 268 基本持平，仍远低于长期均值 3.94%、中位数 4.52%；尽管 AI capex 持续烧钱，信用市场对 AI 风险仍无压力，是当前最支撑多头的指标，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: ICE BofA HY OAS is ~269bps (Convex 269 on July 10, TradingEconomics 269, govspending 271 on July 16; the primary FRED host was egress-blocked, so FRED mirrors are used), essentially flat vs last week's ~268 and far below the 3.94% long-run average and 4.52% median; despite ongoing AI capex burn, credit markets show no stress on AI risk — the most bull-supportive indicator right now, stays green

indicators.name_zh: 数据中心 ABS 利差
indicators.threshold_text: 走阔 50bps+ = 红 / 稳定 = 黄 / 收窄 = 绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widening 50bps+ = red / Stable = yellow / Tightening = green
indicators.note: 数据中心 ABS 条件持续宽松：顶级券利差近两周再收窄约 60bps（回撤速度快于多数其他资产类别），发行创纪录/近纪录、新券供给放缓反令需求回燃（Green Street、GlobalCapital）；信用市场对 AI 基建仍无压力，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Data-center ABS conditions stay accommodative: top-rated spreads tightened another ~60bps over the past couple of weeks (retracing faster than most other asset classes), with record/near-record issuance and slowing new-deal flow reigniting demand (Green Street, GlobalCapital); no credit stress on AI infrastructure — stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 任何违约/降级=红
indicators.threshold_text_en: Any default/downgrade = red
indicators.note: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda / Applied Digital 90 日内无评级下调或违约。CoreWeave 股价月内约 -35%（约 $72-80，Meta 自建云竞争担忧、7/1 单日 -14%，卖方评级下调如 Seeking Alpha『Sell』），但均为股权层面；信用侧最近一次机构动作反而偏正面——S&P 上调 CoreWeave 展望至正面、Moody's 予 CoreWeave 算力 SPV A3。GPU 抵押债与 2026-27 再融资墙仍是潜在隐患，短期零信用事件，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda / Applied Digital — no downgrades or defaults in 90 days. CoreWeave equity is down ~35% on the month (~$72-80, on Meta-compute competition fears, a July 1 -14% session and sell-side cuts like a Seeking Alpha 'Sell'), but those are equity actions; on the credit side the most recent agency move was positive — S&P revised CoreWeave's outlook to positive and Moody's assigned A3 to a CoreWeave compute SPV. GPU-collateralized debt and a 2026-27 refinancing wall remain latent risks — yet with zero near-term credit events it stays green

indicators.name_zh: 全口径债务/Capex 流量比
indicators.threshold_text_en: ≥70% red / 50-70% yellow / <50% green (dot-com peak: 80-100%)
indicators.note: 周度台账：本周新增两笔小额 deal——Nebius 约 $0.78B GPU/资产抵押 SPV 定期贷款（7/10 协议）、General Compute 约 $0.40B 私募信用（Upper90，以 SambaNova SN50 推理 ASIC 为抵押，首笔『芯片抵押』债）；本周无单笔 ≥$10B（上周 Amazon $25B 已计）。另有 CoreWeave 传 7 月借入 $2.6B 供 OpenAI SPV，日期存疑未计入台账、待核。台账仍沿 MS 锚定 2026E 全口径新增债务约 $570B，分母 = 2026E 全口径 compute capex 约 $1.04T，$570B ÷ $1.04T ≈ 55%（区间 50-60%），低于互联网/电信基建顶 80-100%，维持黄灯。距上次完整对账（7/6）13 天，下次约 8/3
indicators.note_en: Weekly ledger: two small new deals this week — Nebius' ~$0.78B GPU/asset-backed SPV term loan (July 10 agreement) and General Compute's ~$0.40B private-credit loan (Upper90, collateralized by SambaNova SN50 inference ASICs, the first 'chip-collateral' debt); no single ≥$10B deal this week (last week's Amazon $25B is already logged). A report that CoreWeave borrowed $2.6B in July for an OpenAI SPV has ambiguous timing and is not yet logged, pending verification. The ledger holds MS's anchor of ~$570B of 2026E all-in new AI debt; denominator = 2026E all-in compute capex ~$1.04T. $570B / $1.04T ≈ 55% (50-60% range), below the 80-100% dot-com/telecom peak — stays yellow. 13 days since the last full reconciliation (July 6); next recon ~Aug 3
indicators.source_name: Morgan Stanley + 自下而上台账

indicators.name_zh: AI 行业 Token 月度环比
indicators.threshold_text: 收缩=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Contraction = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: OpenRouter 约 100T/月、约 25T/周（8M+ 用户，较半年前约 5T/周增约 5x），周度单模型 Tencent HY3 一周约 6.13T（7/13 公布）；Deloitte 称 67% 企业月耗 >10 亿 token。但本期未获独立新月度环比披露、绝对量与上期持平于约 100T/月——核心 value（+35%）已连续 4 期沿用，本期标记 token_volume_mom 疑似静态、待人工核查。行业绝对用量仍处高位扩张、无收缩迹象，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: OpenRouter is ~100T/month and ~25T/week (8M+ users, up ~5x from ~5T/week six months ago), with one model — Tencent's HY3 — at ~6.13T in a single week (reported July 13); Deloitte notes 67% of enterprises consume 1B+ tokens/month. But no independent new monthly print was obtained this period and absolute volume is flat at ~100T/month — the core value (+35%) has now been carried for four straight weeks, so token_volume_mom is flagged suspect-static for manual review. Industry usage remains at a high, expanding level with no sign of contraction — stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 环比跌>15%/供过于求=红 / 阴跌=黄 / 稳定或上涨=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: -15%+ MoM / glut = red / Drifting lower = yellow / Stable-to-rising = green
indicators.note: H100 群组中位约 $3.15/hr（Thunder Compute；on-demand 底约 $2.01、区间 $2.01-11.06），getdeploying 均价约 $3.38；H200 中位约 $4.11/hr（区间 $2.30-13.78）。可得性约 48-53% 在库（约半数售罄/满载），无供给过剩；另见 Silicon Data 记 H100 价约 +10% 小幅上行。本指标为算力供需最直接温度计，价格企稳偏紧、维持绿灯（Blackwell 放量后关注是否转阴跌）
indicators.note_en: H100 cohort median ~$3.15/hr (Thunder Compute; on-demand floor ~$2.01, range $2.01-11.06), getdeploying average ~$3.38; H200 median ~$4.11/hr (range $2.30-13.78). Availability is ~48-53% in stock (about half sold out / at capacity) with no glut, and Silicon Data notes a ~10% H100 price uptick. As the most direct thermometer of compute supply-demand, prices are firm and tight — stays green (watch for a drift lower once Blackwell ramps)

indicators.name_zh: AI 收入增速的二阶导
indicators.threshold_text: 减速=红 / 平稳=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Decel = red / Flat = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Anthropic ARR 报约 $47B（5 月，随 $65B Series H / $965B post 披露；部分口径给出更高的年化跑量约 $69B，基准存争议），4 月约 $30B→5 月约 $47B、单月增约 $17B，FY26 预测 $75-90B；OpenAI 约 $33B run-rate（2 月约 $25B）。月增量持续放大、二阶导维持正向，唯口径（含云分销毛收入）仍存争议，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Anthropic's ARR is reported at ~$47B (May, disclosed with the $65B Series H / $965B post-money; some bases cite a higher ~$69B annualized run-rate, and the basis is contested), rising from ~$30B in April to ~$47B in May (~$17B added in a month), with FY26 projected at $75-90B; OpenAI is ~$33B run-rate (from ~$25B in February). The monthly increment keeps expanding and the 2nd derivative stays positive, though the accounting basis (gross cloud-reseller revenue) remains contested — stays green

indicators.threshold_text_en: <50% red / 50-65% yellow / >65% green
indicators.note: McKinsey Q1 2026：72% 企业至少 1 项 AI workload 生产化（2024 为 55%、2020 为 20%），5000+ 人企业达 83%、88% 在至少一个职能用 AI；Deloitte 2026 报告称 ≥40% 项目进入生产的公司数半年内将翻倍。头部部署率高位、深度集成仍在爬坡，维持绿灯（季度调查，沿用 Q1）
indicators.note_en: McKinsey Q1 2026: 72% of enterprises run at least one AI workload in production (vs 55% in 2024, 20% in 2020), rising to 83% at firms with 5,000+ employees, with 88% using AI in at least one function; Deloitte's 2026 report sees the share of firms with 40%+ of projects in production doubling within six months. The headline rate is high while deep integration keeps climbing — stays green (quarterly survey, Q1 carried)

indicators.threshold_text: 负增长=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Negative = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Oracle FY26 Q4 RPO 约 $638B（+363% YoY，环比 +$85B，其中约 $75B 为客户自备 GPU 的预付 AI 合约）；Microsoft 商业 RPO 约 $627B（+99% YoY，截至 3/31，约 30% 12 个月内确认、除 OpenAI 外仍增约 26%）；Google Cloud 积压约 $462B。大三合计积压 >$1.7T（超上期 >$1.4T 口径），订单强劲；唯 Oracle 逾 50% RPO 来自 OpenAI、客户集中并存，维持绿灯（MSFT/Google 为 3/31 季度，最新已披露）
indicators.note_en: Oracle FY26 Q4 RPO is ~$638B (+363% YoY, +$85B sequentially, ~$75B of it prepaid AI contracts with customer-supplied GPUs); Microsoft's commercial RPO is ~$627B (+99% YoY as of March 31, ~30% recognized within 12 months, +26% ex-OpenAI); Google Cloud backlog is ~$462B. Big-3 backlog tops $1.7T (above last period's >$1.4T basis) and the order book is strong; but over 50% of Oracle's RPO is from OpenAI, so concentration coexists with strong demand — stays green (MSFT/Google are March 31 quarters, the latest filed)

indicators.threshold_text: 停滞=红 / 放缓=黄 / 正常或加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Stalled = red / Decelerating = yellow / On-trend or accelerating = green
indicators.note: 前沿进展仍在轨且偏加速：METR 时程倍增自 2023 后约 129 天（4.3 月）、2024 起数据约 89 天（3 月），前沿 50% 成功率时程约 12-14 小时（Claude Opus 4.6 约 719 分）；近 90 天旗舰密集发布——Claude Opus 4.8（5/28）、Claude Sonnet 5（6/30）、Claude Fable 5（首个 Mythos 级、刷新 HLE 无工具 53.3% 与 SWE-bench Pro 80.3% SOTA、居 Artificial Analysis 指数第一）、GPT-5.6（7/9 转默认）。capex 第一性支撑（模型持续变好解锁场景）完好，维持绿灯（对比台账上期无实质放缓）
indicators.note_en: Frontier progress stays on-trend and if anything accelerating: METR's time-horizon doubling is ~129 days (4.3 months) post-2023 and ~89 days (3 months) on 2024+ data, with the frontier 50% horizon ~12-14 hours (Claude Opus 4.6 ~719 min); flagship releases clustered in the last 90 days — Claude Opus 4.8 (May 28), Claude Sonnet 5 (June 30), Claude Fable 5 (the first Mythos-class model, resetting HLE-no-tools SOTA to 53.3% and SWE-bench Pro to 80.3%, #1 on the Artificial Analysis index), and GPT-5.6 (default July 9). The first-principles support for capex (models keep improving, unlocking use cases) is intact — stays green (no material slowdown versus the prior ledger reading)

indicators.threshold_text: 任何=红 / 调查=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Any confirmed = red / Investigation = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 本周无核心 AI 龙头新增正式执法：现有 AI 相关 SEC 案件均较旧且针对非核心/私营标的（Presto Automation AI-washing 2025/1、Nate Inc. 2025/4 诈骗）；SEC 执法部门 2026 持续关注 AI-washing 与会计造假（属表态而非新案）。Burry 持续指控 Mag7 借延长 GPU 折旧虚增利润，属观点非执法；核心龙头未触及，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: No new formal enforcement against a core AI bellwether this week: existing AI-related SEC cases are older and against non-core/private names (Presto Automation AI-washing in Jan 2025, Nate Inc. fraud in Apr 2025); the SEC Enforcement Division continues to flag AI-washing and accounting fraud in 2026 (commentary, not a new case). Michael Burry keeps alleging the Mag7 inflate profits via extended GPU depreciation — a view, not an enforcement action; core leaders remain untouched — stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 加息=红 / 通胀压力=黄 / 降息=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Hike = red / Inflation pressure = yellow / Cut = green
indicators.note: Fed 隐含加息 thesis 本周逆转：CME FedWatch（7/18）显示 7/29 FOMC 约 86.7% 维持，12 月约 78.2% 维持 / 15.4% 降息 / 仅 5.4% 加息（上周曾隐含约 77% 12 月前加息）。但通胀仍粘（5 月 CPI 4.2%、中东能源压力、就业稳健→higher-for-longer），政策属『维持高利率(通胀压力)』而非降息，由红降黄。（源间不一致：CNBC 7/13 曾称 7 月加息概率上升，本期取最新 7/18 FedWatch 快照。）
indicators.note_en: The Fed's implied-hike thesis reversed this week: CME FedWatch (July 18) shows ~86.7% hold at the July 29 FOMC and, for December, ~78.2% hold / 15.4% cut / just 5.4% hike (versus last week's implied ~77% chance of a hike by December). But inflation is still sticky (May CPI 4.2%, Middle East energy pressure, solid jobs → higher-for-longer), so policy sits at 'higher-for-longer hold (inflation pressure)' rather than cutting — red to yellow. (Sources disagree: CNBC on July 13 flagged rising July-hike odds; this period uses the latest July 18 FedWatch snapshot.)

indicators.name_zh: 资本开支指引市场反应
indicators.threshold_text: 系统性惩罚=红 / 偶发=黄 / 奖励=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Systematic punishment = red / Episodic = yellow / Rewarded = green
indicators.note: Mag7 Q2 财报 7/22-30 才出（Alphabet 7/22、MSFT/Meta 7/29、Amazon 7/30），本期无新反应，沿用 Q1 的『选择性惩罚』——有云变现的被奖励（Alphabet/Amazon 走高、Google Cloud 强劲），无云变现的被罚（Meta capex 升至 $125-145B 却因无广告外变现被抛售）。本周新信号：TSMC Q2 全面超预期，AI 芯片复合体却仍下跌（beat-and-sell-off），Invezz 7/18 称市场要 $700B AI 支出的『回报证据』。市场由一致奖励转为选择性审视，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Mag7 Q2 earnings land July 22-30 (Alphabet 7/22, MSFT/Meta 7/29, Amazon 7/30), so there is no fresh reaction this period and Q1's 'selective punishment' is carried — names that monetize via cloud were rewarded (Alphabet/Amazon rose, Google Cloud strong), those that cannot were punished (Meta's capex rose to $125-145B but it sold off, lacking non-ad monetization). New signal this week: TSMC beat every Q2 metric yet the AI-chip complex still fell (beat-and-sell-off), and Invezz on July 18 said the market wants 'receipts' on the $700B AI spend. The market has shifted from uniform reward to selective scrutiny — stays yellow

indicators.threshold_text: 普遍承认过热=红 / 部分=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widespread acknowledgment = red / Partial = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 对冲表态持续加深但未成一致：Satya Nadella（7/13）警示 AI 若红利不能广泛惠及将失『social permission』、『若只谈科技公司本身就是泡沫的迹象』；Mark Zuckerberg（约 7/2 内部会）承认 AI agent『未按管理层预期加速』；Altman 称小盘 AI 估值『insane、非理性』、『必有人巨亏』；Dimon 称市场处『bubble territory / 亢奋』、焦虑很高。但均坚持 AI 长期兑现（Pichai 视为供给约束而非需求担忧、上修 capex 至 $180-190B），CEO 未集体收手，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Hedging is deepening but not universal: Satya Nadella (July 13) warned AI could lose its 'social permission' if the benefits are not widely shared and that 'talking only about the tech firms is a tell-tale sign of a bubble'; Mark Zuckerberg (~July 2 internal meeting) admitted AI agents 'haven't accelerated the way leadership expected'; Altman calls small-cap AI valuations 'insane, not rational' and warns 'someone will get burned'; Dimon says the market is in 'bubble territory / exuberance' with anxiety high. Yet all insist AI pays off long term (Pichai frames it as a supply constraint, not a demand worry, and raised capex to $180-190B), and CEOs have not collectively pulled back — stays yellow
